title: Create E2E test: backspace input handling
notes: Type 'git status', backspace to clear, type 'echo hello world', verify output. Tests that backspace is properly forwarded to PTY. Reference: tui-testing RESEARCH_SUMMARY.md backspace test.

title: Create Rust project scaffold with ratatui/crossterm
notes: Initialize Cargo.toml with dependencies: ratatui, crossterm, color-eyre. Create basic main.rs that initializes terminal, runs event loop, and handles Ctrl+Q to quit. Reference: ratatui/examples/apps/hello-world/

title: Integrate portable-pty for spawning shell
notes: Add portable-pty dependency. Create PTY pair, spawn shell (bash/zsh) with current working directory. Set up reader thread to pipe PTY output to main thread via channel. Reference: portable-pty examples/bash.rs, terminal-emulation RESEARCH_SUMMARY.md.

title: Review: Basic TUI with terminal emulation implementation
notes: Review all implementation issues. Verify: all tests pass, code is clean, no skipped tests, E2E tests run in Apple terminal, Ctrl+Q quits properly, layout matches spec exactly.

title: Clone and study Rust terminal multiplexer projects
notes: Clone the source code of existing Rust terminal projects that do similar things to what we're building. Focus on: zellij (terminal multiplexer), wezterm (terminal emulator), rio (terminal emulator). Study their architecture for embedding terminals within a TUI. Save to references/existing-projects/

title: Implement input forwarding from crossterm to PTY
notes: Convert crossterm KeyEvent to terminal escape sequences. Handle special keys: arrows (ESC[A/B/C/D), backspace (0x7f), enter (0x0d), Ctrl+C (0x03), tab. Forward to PTY writer. Reference: zellij terminal_pane.rs key constants.

title: Build CLI and link globally as 'sb'
notes: Configure Cargo.toml [[bin]] section with name 'sb'. Build release binary. Install globally with cargo install --path . or symlink to PATH. Verify 'sb' command works from any directory.

title: Create E2E test: layout matches spec
notes: Add terminal-testlib dev dependency. Test that sidebar is 20 chars wide, title row is blue with centered 'Sidebar TUI' in black, sidebar body has lighter background. Reference: tui-testing RESEARCH_SUMMARY.md test examples.

title: Research terminal emulation libraries in Rust
notes: Research and clone docs/code for Rust libraries that handle terminal emulation and PTY management. Focus on: portable-pty, vt100, termwiz, pty-process. Need to understand how to create a pseudo-terminal, run shell commands, and capture/render output. Save to references/terminal-emulation/

title: Handle terminal resize events
notes: Detect crossterm Event::Resize. Update PTY size with master.resize(PtySize). Update vt100 parser dimensions with parser.set_size(). Recalculate layout constraints. Reference: wezterm resize handling.

title: Add vt100 parser for terminal emulation
notes: Add vt100 dependency. Create Parser with terminal dimensions. Feed PTY output to parser. Implement rendering from vt100::Screen to ratatui Paragraph widget with proper color mapping. Reference: vt100 examples, terminal-emulation RESEARCH_SUMMARY.md.

title: Create E2E test: vi editing workflow
notes: Open vi in TUI terminal, edit a file, save it, exit. Verify file contents changed. Test insert mode, escape, :wq sequence. Reference: tui-testing RESEARCH_SUMMARY.md vi test example.

title: Implement sidebar layout with 20-char fixed width
notes: Create Layout::horizontal with Constraint::Length(20) for sidebar and Fill(1) for terminal area. Add blue header row with 'Sidebar TUI' centered in black text. Sidebar body should have a lighter background color. Reference: ratatui demo ui.rs, constraints example.

title: Research E2E testing strategies for TUI applications
notes: Figure out how agents can automatically test a TUI that requires user input and interaction. Research: expectrl, rexpect, termion testing, scripted terminal automation. Need to test in a real Apple terminal environment. The objectives explicitly say to figure this out BEFORE building the TUI. Save to references/tui-testing/

title: Research Rust TUI frameworks (ratatui, crossterm, cursive)
notes:
## Research Complete

**Recommendation: Use ratatui + crossterm**

### Cloned Repositories:
- ratatui: references/tui-frameworks/ratatui/
- crossterm: references/tui-frameworks/crossterm/
- cursive: references/tui-frameworks/cursive/

### Key Findings:
1. **ratatui** is the best choice for building the TUI layout (sidebar + terminal view)
2. Uses crossterm as backend for cross-platform support
3. Layout system with Constraint::Length(20) matches our sidebar requirement perfectly
4. Ctrl+Q detection via crossterm's KeyModifiers::CONTROL + KeyCode::Char('q')

### Relevant Examples Reviewed:
- ratatui/examples/apps/hello-world/ - Basic app structure
- ratatui/examples/apps/demo/src/ui.rs - Complex layouts with sidebars
- ratatui/examples/apps/constraints/ - All constraint types
- crossterm/examples/event-match-modifiers.rs - Ctrl+key handling

### Summary Document:
See references/tui-frameworks/RESEARCH_SUMMARY.md for detailed implementation notes and code patterns.

title: Create E2E test: git status output matches normal terminal
notes: Run 'git status' in TUI terminal. Compare output with baseline from running git status directly. Test in the project directory which is a git repo. Reference: tui-testing RESEARCH_SUMMARY.md.
